basics.name: Anders Bo Rasmussen
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Software Engineer with 10+ years of experience building backend systems, platform tooling, and AI-powered products, primarily in C# and TypeScript. Experienced driving architecture decisions and technical scoping in cross-functional product teams, from data model design through production infrastructure. Increasingly focused on the intersection of AI and developer tooling, including open-source work on multi-agent workflows and Model Context Protocol integrations. Known for raising the technical bar of the teams I work in through mentoring, knowledge sharing, and acting as a technical lead where the role calls for it.

work.name: LEGO Group
work.summary: Working on the Creative Work Management platform, focused on AI-powered tooling for marketing and campaign operations. Drove architecture decisions, led cross-team technical initiatives, and raised the technical bar of the team through mentoring and knowledge sharing.
highlights: Led end-to-end delivery of Smart Briefing from architecture design through production deployment on AWS, covering AI-driven brief generation, deliverable predictions, schema versioning, and infrastructure provisioning.
Authored ADR-001, defining a JSONB-based storage-agnostic model for deliverable predictions with field-level reasoning and schema versioning.
Led technical scoping and cross-team coordination for a multi-epic platform initiative, driving the decomposition of a 20-story epic into independently deliverable tracks.
Provisioned the Smart Briefing production environment on AWS using Terraform, including drift investigation and resolution between dev and prod configurations.
Coached junior and mid-level engineers through structured sessions on scoping and technical decision-making, operating as a technical lead across these interactions.
Published a weekly internal AI newsletter covering developments relevant to the engineering team.

work.name: Siteimprove
work.summary: Focused on modernising the platform architecture through micro frontends, and contributed to AI-powered content tooling. Worked across both infrastructure and product features.
highlights: Built the internal Micro Frontend framework in TypeScript, enabling independent deployment and scalability across the platform.
Contributed to an AI-agnostic tone-of-voice validation application written in TypeScript, consuming AI capabilities via a shared internal endpoint.
Developed and maintained shared components and services across a polyglot stack (Rust, C#, TypeScript).

work.name: DFDS A/S
work.summary: Backend engineer on the Planning system for DFDS Logistics, a C#/WPF desktop application with a C#/MSSQL backend. Focused on code quality, knowledge sharing, and team onboarding.
highlights: Migrated a PostgreSQL database from version 10 to version 15 in AWS RDS, upgrading the underlying server tier from 2 to 3.
Hosted tech talks and championed dedicated learning time across the team.
Part of the buddy system, ensuring new employees got a strong start at DFDS.

work.name: Hesehus A/S
work.summary: Worked on e-commerce solutions built in C#, ASP.NET, and ASP.NET Core, with Razor Pages and React.js frontends. Focused primarily on performance improvements across search and data layers.
highlights: Upgraded older Lucene indexes to Elasticsearch, increasing performance and maintainability.
Integrated e-commerce solutions with the BizzKit platform.
Optimised slow-running queries and introduced multilayered caching using Redis.

work.name: Itera
work.summary: Consultant responsible for an emailing system and a custom backup solution built to meet strict compliance requirements. Stack: C#, ASP.NET, Knockout.js, Azure Functions, and Azure Blob Storage.
highlights: Extended the emailing system with threaded conversation support.
Built an Outlook add-in connecting Outlook with Jira, enabling ticket creation and issue viewing directly from email.
Built a custom backup system using Azure Functions and Azure Blob Storage.

work.name: Sigma Estimates A/S
work.summary: Part of the Core Sigma Team, working on the build pipeline, cloud framework, and SharePoint Online integration for the Sigma Ecosystem. Cloud and integration work written in C#.
highlights: Implemented a new cloud framework for the Sigma Ecosystem using CQRS and Event Sourcing.
Created a custom SharePoint Online file handler, enabling users to open files directly in the Sigma Engine.
Integrated third-party pricing catalogues into the Sigma Engine.
Migrated the build pipeline to new server hardware.

work.name: ALD Automotive
work.summary: Rebuilt the customer portal MyALD from scratch using C#, ASP.NET Core MVC, and MSSQL, replacing a legacy Sitecore solution.
highlights: Led the migration from Sitecore to a custom ASP.NET Core MVC web application.
Integrated the backend with a new frontend delivered by In2Media.
Took over and maintained frontend development from the external vendor.

work.name: Autobutler
work.summary: Joined as an intern and progressed to Junior Software Developer. Built and maintained the Autobutler platform, written in Ruby on Rails with a CoffeeScript/Angular.js frontend migrating to React.js.
highlights: Integrated the platform with third-party services including Comparo (insurance) and Uber.
Built a car insurance offer solution in partnership with NeXt Insurance.
Designed and implemented a customer benefit system for Autobutler users.
Refactored the in-house CMS for improved performance, maintainability, and usability.

volunteer.summary: Denmark's biggest music festival. I was part of the crew making sure that internet and telephony was working throughout the festival.
Sørby eSport is a local eSport club. I was part of the crew making sure that events ran smoothly.

references.name: Hadi Salameh
references.reference: It's good to have Anders on the team as he always puts a smile on your face which is necessary when you are dealing with tedious logistical problems. He always offers his help and is just as humble to request it when he needs it. A true champ!

projects.name: AIchemist
projects.description: Open-source AI developer tooling plugin for Claude Code and GitHub Copilot CLI, featuring 7 specialized agents and 19 workflow skills covering code review, Jira execution, architecture exploration, Playwright testing, PostgreSQL querying, Obsidian research, and Microsoft 365 calendar workflows.
highlights: Designed multi-agent review and implementation workflows with safety gates, confidence scoring, Jira integration, and domain-specific guidance for .NET, TypeScript/React, and DDD.
Productized with installable manifests, documentation, GitHub Actions validation, automated release management, and marketplace metadata.
Added transcript-mining hooks that turn repeated manual patterns into structured automation candidates.

projects.name: AIchemist UI
projects.description: Desktop AI coding assistant that connects to local project directories and runs LLM agent sessions with file access, shell execution, approval-gated tools, and persistent chat history.
highlights: Architected an Electron + React desktop app with typed IPC, SQLite persistence, and Zustand state management for streaming agent sessions, tool calls, and session hydration.
Integrated multiple AI providers including Anthropic Claude, GitHub Copilot, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible endpoints, with provider probing, model selection, and session continuity.
Built approval-gated MCP/tool execution for file writes, shell commands, web fetches, and native transcript rendering.
Maintained 75+ Vitest test files covering Electron IPC, provider integrations, database persistence, renderer state, and UI flows.
